free_text: Measurement of the 159Tb(n, gamma) cross section at
Neutrons were produced via the spallation
reactions by bombarding a massive tungsten target
with a 1.6 GeV proton beam, operating at a 25 Hz
repetition rate, and collimated to a 30 mm diameter
beam spot at the sample position.
The neutron energies ranging from thermal
to a few hundred MeV were determined using the time
of flight (TOF) method with a 76 m long flight path.
Saturated resonance technique with the 197Au 4.9 eV
The neutron capture yields were studied using
the total energy measurement principle based on an
array of four C6D6 detectors with the pulse height
The neutron flux in the energy range of
1 eV to 100 MeV was obtained with a combination of
the data from dedicated measurements of the 6LiF-
silicon (Li-Si) detector array and the 235U loaded
multilayer fission chamber. The Li-Si detector neutron
flux was normalized to 235U data at 10-20 keV since
its position was about 20 m away from the sample.
A metallic terbium sample of natural abundance with a
thickness of 0.2 mm was used for the 159Tb(n,g)
reaction study. The 197Au sample was used as the
standard for calculating the relative cross section
of 159Tb(n,g) in neutron energies ranging from 2
keV to 1 MeV. The natPb samples were used to
